channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Car parked on double red line dumping multiple black bin bags of waste in the council bins, 12th March
item.description:
I was passing by and saw an altercation between a three people. A man and a woman (white English in their 50s I’d say) with a small fiat car reg BIL 1674 (or B1L 1674) were parked on double red line unloading multiple big black waste bags into the council bins for the flats above (on West Lane). A younger man was filming them and I think had confronted them. The woman then started filming and shouting at this younger man, who walked into the communal door of block of flats. The woman film him going in, held the door open and waited to see which flat he went into. The other man was looking too. It was quite intimidating for the younger man, from what I saw at least. I stood and watched and the situation calmed a little. I took the number plate and headed home.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dixon's Alley, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly Tipping on Street problem has been found, 12th March
item.description:
A Fly Tipping on Street problem has been found by Hackney Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mount Pleasant Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footpath adjacent to Morley Court beginning to break up, 12th March
item.description:
For some months now there has been water leaking from a outflow in the communal gardens of the above-mentioned block of flats. This is, of course, a Thames Water issue between the company and the residents. However, in view of the time this has been going on the condition of the pavement is deteriorating. The fault was also a danger during freezing weather when the water froze.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72, Westmoreland Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: New bollard required ASAP, 12th March
item.description:
This issue was raised six months ago following a serious car accident, but nothing has been done yet, despite the matter being reported twice. Pedestrian safety is crucial, and a visible bollard needs to be installed as a matter of urgency. At the moment there are only two temporary cones, which are not fixed in place and keep moving around in the wind, making the situation even more dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 319, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Multiple cracks on New N Popham Rd intersection, 12th March

item.title: Loads of domestic rubbish dumped (including bills), 12th March
item.description:
Loads of rubbish and domestic waste has been unlawfully dumped outside the flats at 68 High Street, Penge. It's a health hazard and is disgusting. It includes a lot of post from a particular house on nearby close.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Penge Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sunk drain Essex Rd Bus Stop A, 12th March
item.description:
In front of the bin. It's sunk so deep a cyclist could face plant onto the ground. Rest of the bus lane further west isn't great either. Lots of pot holes for busses, taxis and bikes to avoid. Needs attention.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Essex Road, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All over, 12th March
item.description:
All over
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Shooters Hill Road, Kidbrooke, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)
